sentence_reviews.text: Evaluation-driven development makes tests, risk thresholds, regression suites, and release gates part of the iterative model and application lifecycle.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF 1.0 Govern/Map/Measure/Manage functions and AI RMF webpage overview on incorporating trustworthiness into AI design, development, use, and evaluation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Narrowed to release-gate evidence, regression checks, risk thresholds, and monitoring rather than downstream substrate claims.

sentence_reviews.text: Evaluation-driven development uses test evidence, risk thresholds, and regression results to decide whether a model or AI application should advance, roll back, or require more review.

sentence_reviews.text: Release gates can miss novel failures when the test suite, threat model, or monitored metric does not cover the deployment context.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF 1.0 Govern/Map/Measure/Manage functions and AI RMF webpage overview on incorporating trustworthiness into AI design, development, use, and evaluation.

sentence_reviews.text: Evaluation-driven development uses benchmarks, tests, and task metrics as evidence for release or rollback decisions.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF 1.0 Govern/Map/Measure/Manage functions and AI RMF webpage overview on incorporating trustworthiness into AI design, development, use, and evaluation.

sentence_reviews.text: Evaluation-driven release gates use red-team and threat-model findings to decide whether residual risk is acceptable.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF 1.0 Govern/Map/Measure/Manage functions and AI RMF webpage overview on incorporating trustworthiness into AI design, development, use, and evaluation.

sentence_reviews.text: Evaluation-driven development can include human evaluation and preference studies when automated metrics do not capture quality or risk.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF 1.0 Govern/Map/Measure/Manage functions and AI RMF webpage overview on incorporating trustworthiness into AI design, development, use, and evaluation.

sentence_reviews.text: Evaluation-driven release processes use reproducibility checks, drift monitoring, and operational signals after deployment.
